assumptions: No Dropbox account, app, app name, content-access choice, scope, app key, app secret, redirect URI, access token, Node.js project, SDK installation, environment variable, or Dropbox files are assumed.
A supported computer, browser, terminal, email or available Dropbox identity method, and Node.js 22 or newer are available.
No signup, identity authorization, verification, app creation, permission selection, token generation, package installation, or API request was performed during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Dropbox account, create an app in the Developer Console for own-account testing, select minimal content access and the `files.metadata.read` permission, generate an access token, install the JavaScript SDK, and list the root folder from Node.js.

first_success.outcome: The Node.js `filesListFolder({ path: '' })` call resolves and the example logs the returned `result.entries` array for the account's root folder.
first_success.observable_signal: The promise resolves and prints `result.entries`; an empty array still proves the authenticated read succeeded, while `catch(console.error)` receives failures.

route_selection.surface: Dropbox JavaScript SDK generated-token root-folder list
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Dropbox JavaScript SDK generated-token root-folder list
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Standard OAuth authorization-code flow
candidates.reason: Dropbox recommends code flow for real applications and other users, but the same guide explicitly provides generated tokens for quick own-account API testing before OAuth implementation.

candidates.name: Browser PKCE flow
candidates.reason: PKCE is the secure browser route, but it adds a browser app, Web Crypto, authorization redirect, and token exchange before the first API result.

candidates.name: Chooser, Saver, or Embedder
candidates.reason: These pre-built web components do not require an OAuth token, but they are a different integration surface from the API and SDK first call.

required_path.action: Complete Dropbox account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Dropbox account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Dropbox candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The promise resolves and prints `result.entries`; an empty array still proves the authenticated read succeeded, while `catch(console.error)` receives failures.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create or access a Dropbox account that the test app can read.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S3', 'S4']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'configuration', 'requirement': 'Create a new app in the Dropbox App Console and choose its content access, including App Folder or Full Dropbox.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S4']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'Enable the least required scope for the test call; `filesListFolder` requires `files.metadata.read`.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S5']}

external_gates.description: A Dropbox account is required, but the signup page's fields and completion sequence are client-rendered and unavailable publicly.
The app-creation URL redirects to login before any Developer Console fields or token controls are available.
App Folder and Full Dropbox are peer content-access choices with different data reach.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Use the Dropbox JavaScript SDK to list files in the root of the developer's Dropbox account.

excluded.item: Pagination with `filesListFolderContinue`, file upload or download, webhooks, OAuth for other users, refresh-token handling, app review, production distribution, team administration, and synchronizati
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Dropbox show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
